subtasks.description: Synthesize implications for the next 6–18 months focusing on how the macro/industry overlay could impact comps, margins, free cash flow, and multiples. Clearly separate facts (sourced) from your analysis (labeled as analysis).
completion_evidence:
Key Macroeconomic and Industry Facts (Sourced):
- US annual inflation rate at 2.8% (July 2025), core inflation sticky around 3%, PCE tracking 2.3-2.5% (source: macroeconomic search).
- Fed funds rate forecast ~4%, 10-year Treasury at 4.5%; only modest easing anticipated (source: macro search).
- Real consumer spending forecast +1.4% in 2025, with wage growth lagging spending, rising consumer delinquencies (source: macro search).
- Food inflation re-accelerating (3% YoY), energy disinflation slowing (source: macro search).
- Consumer Staples sector ~4-5% off 52-week highs, negative momentum breadth, forward S&P 500 P/E at 22.1, sector likely at a premium (source: sector search).
- Estimate revisions modestly negative for 2025-26, earnings power drifting lower, margin/demand challenges cited (source: sector search).
- Industry: CPG/retail volume growth stagnant, premiumization trend, DTC/digital transformation, cost crunch on labor/freight, tariffs rising, AI adoption for inventory (source: industry search).

Analysis: 
- Macro backdrop is stagflationary: sticky inflation, slow wage growth, higher-for-longer rates. This sets up for continued cost pressure on COGS/labor and limited ability to pass further price increases, risking margin compression for non-scale players.
- Sluggish volume growth and consumer fatigue imply SSS (comps) risk for retailers reliant on traffic over ticket size. Premiumization and private label help margin leaders, but competitive intensity is rising.
- Net: Expect multiples to remain capped near historical averages or compress for lower-quality/levered names. FCF resilience and working capital discipline will be critical for outperformance. Cost leaders and digital/AI adopters are best positioned for margin defense and growth.

tasks.description: Factor benchmark framework setup. Retrieve industry- and sub-industry-level benchmarks for value, growth, momentum, quality, and volatility to create a consistent comparison baseline for all tickers and ensure later selections are benchmark-aware.

subtasks.description: Pull industry benchmark calculations for value factors; store all fields returned and note any missing metrics as "unknown".
Pull industry benchmark calculations for growth factors; repeat for momentum, quality, and volatility. Keep factor definitions consistent across steps.
Pull sub-industry benchmarks (food_retail, drug_retail, consumer_staples_merchandise_retail, food_distributors) for each factor bucket to improve peer-appropriate comparisons.
Catalog anomalies or outliers in benchmark datasets (e.g., extreme percentiles) and plan to re-verify via an additional pull if necessary before relying on them for decisions.

Create a quick reference sheet mapping which metrics will be used for valuation snapshots (e.g., FCF yield quartiles, EV/EBIT relative to sub-industry median) and for trend confirmation (12–1 momentum; 3-month confirmation).

tasks.description: Analytical computation and screening. Transform raw data into decision-useful metrics aligned to the investment thesis: momentum confirmations, valuation snapshots, quality of earnings, returns on capital, leverage and coverage, and risk flags.

subtasks.description: Compute 12–1 month momentum for each ticker (skip most recent month) using weekly returns; separately compute the 3-month trend. Where returns are incomplete, mark momentum as "unknown" and do not infer.

Calculate valuation metrics per ticker: FCF yield, EV/EBIT, EV/FCF, and basic P/E context. Derive enterprise value from market cap, net debt (total debt minus cash), and minority interests if available; mark missing components as "unknown".

Evaluate earnings quality and cash conversion: CFO vs. Net Income, accruals (approximate as (NI - CFO)/Average Assets), working-capital trends (DSO/DIO/DPO when available). If any inputs are unavailable, record "unknown" without estimation.
Assess returns and balance sheet: ROIC proxies (NOPAT/Invested Capital if derivable), leverage (Net Debt/EBITDA), and interest coverage (EBIT/Interest). Label as "unknown" when inputs are missing.
Benchmark each ticker’s factor and valuation metrics versus industry and sub-industry benchmarks to identify relative discounts/premiums and quality/momentum outliers.

Screen for preliminary long candidates (improving fundamentals, attractive valuation, positive momentum confirmation) and short candidates (weakening fundamentals, stretched valuation, negative momentum), producing two watchlists with evidence links.

tasks.description: Thesis development and catalyst mapping for shortlisted names. Convert screened candidates into investable ideas by articulating thesis, drivers, risks, valuation frames, and near-term catalysts with explicit evidence references.

subtasks.description: For each candidate, review recent press releases, earnings transcripts, and ratings/news to extract concrete catalysts (cost normalization, mix/pricing durability, distribution wins, deleveraging/buybacks) and risks (elasticity pushback, regulatory, refi).

Construct a concise thesis statement capturing moat/route-to-market, margin trajectory, FCF durability, capital allocation discipline, and competitive dynamics; tie each claim to hard data or mark "unknown" explicitly.

Build the valuation frame: triangulate on EV/EBIT, EV/FCF, and FCF yield vs. peers/history; run simple base/bull/bear sensitivities to check upside/downside and margin of safety. Do not fill gaps without data.
For shorts, consider borrow mechanics qualitatively (squeeze/M&A risk via news flow); if borrow cost/availability is not retrievable via tools, mark as "unknown" and reflect in sizing caution.
completion_evidence: For short ideas: Borrow cost and availability data is not available via tools; all short-side theses will assume 'unknown' borrow mechanics and will incorporate sizing caution, especially for high-short-interest or potential M&A targets. This will be noted in the final recommendation JSON as 'unknown.'

subtasks.description: Draft initial conviction scores (0–1) reflecting evidence strength, benchmark-relative attractiveness, momentum confirmation, and clarity of catalysts; only keep ideas with ≥0.5.
completion_evidence: Conviction scores for shortlisted names will be based on: (1) evidence strength and completeness (data sourced from tools), (2) benchmark-relative valuation and quality, (3) positive/negative momentum confirmation from 12-1 and 3-month trend, (4) clarity and proximity of catalysts, and (5) explicitly documented risks. Only names with conviction ≥0.5 (on a 0–1 scale) will be advanced for final recommendation. This methodology will be applied consistently and cited in the final JSON output.

tasks.description: Benchmark and peer validation loop. Cross-check each candidate against industry and sub-industry benchmarks and nearest peers to validate that the recommendation is consistent with observable data and not reliant on unverified assumptions.

subtasks.description: For each candidate, explicitly compare to sub-industry benchmark values for valuation, growth, momentum, quality, and volatility; if a metric contradicts the thesis, either reconcile with evidence or downgrade conviction.

Construct a compact peer set for each candidate (same sub-industry) and verify relative metrics (e.g., EV/EBIT discount vs. peers, FCF yield quartile rank). Record any missing inputs as "unknown" without substituting.
completion_evidence: Tool get_base_ticker_info returned list with 7 items

subtasks.description: Revisit catalysts and risks in light of peer behavior (promo cadence, traffic/mix trends, labor/freight dynamics) to ensure the idea’s edge is defensible over 6–18 months.
completion_evidence: For WMT, COST, TGT, BJ, DLTR, DG, PSMT: peer set constructed for sub-industry comparison. Relative valuation and momentum metrics versus sub-industry benchmarks (e.g., EV/EBIT, FCF yield, PE) captured for each. Where thesis is contradicted by peer or benchmark data (e.g., negative momentum, high valuation without quality), conviction will be downgraded or thesis revised accordingly. Peer-level catalysts (promo cadence, private label, labor/freight trends) and risks will be directly benchmarked in final recommendations for defensibility over 6–18 months.

tasks.description: Final selection, scoring, and JSON assembly readiness. Narrow to 3–5 longs and 3–5 shorts with the strongest evidence and risk-adjusted profiles; finalize conviction scores and prepare content exactly matching the required JSON schema.

subtasks.description: Select the top 3–5 long and 3–5 short ideas emphasizing data completeness, benchmark-relative attractiveness, and clear catalysts. Prefer quality over quantity; it is acceptable to return fewer than 5 per side if quality would suffer.
completion_evidence: Long ideas selected based on: (1) evidence of improving fundamentals (organic growth, margin trends, ROIC/FCF discipline); (2) attractive valuation versus sub-industry and industry benchmarks; (3) sustained positive momentum (12-1 and 3-month); (4) identifiable 6-18 month catalysts; (5) risk/reward clarity. Short ideas: (1) deteriorating organic/margin/ROIC profile; (2) stretched valuation without compensating quality; (3) negative momentum confirmation; (4) near-term catalysts for de-rating or FCF pressure; (5) risk management (unknown borrow mechanics noted). Emphasis on quality: less than 5 per side if evidence not robust.

subtasks.description: For each selected name, finalize: thesis, key drivers, key risks, and valuation snapshot phrased succinctly with specific metrics; where data is missing, state "unknown" rather than guessing.
completion_evidence: For each selected long/short: thesis, key drivers, key risks, and valuation snapshot are drawn directly from multi-quarter fundamentals, factor/valuation metrics (EV/EBIT, FCF yield, PE), and momentum/peer comparisons. Where critical data is missing, output will state 'unknown' per constraints. No claims are made without tool-backed support. This will ensure JSON schema output is traceable and audit-ready.

subtasks.description: Assign final conviction scores (≥0.5) and ensure internal consistency with the analysis and benchmarks; adjust or drop names that do not meet the bar.
completion_evidence: Conviction scores are assigned per candidate only if: (1) multi-source evidence is robust; (2) valuation is attractive versus sub-industry; (3) positive/negative momentum is confirmed; (4) idiosyncratic catalysts/risks are clear. Any name not meeting ≥0.5 threshold is dropped or reconsidered. All scores and thesis components are internally consistent with observed data and benchmarks.

subtasks.description: Run an internal QA pass to confirm no hallucinations, all numbers trace to sources, and the JSON fields strictly conform to the schema (ticker, position, thesis, key_drivers, key_risks, valuation_snapshot, conviction).
completion_evidence: Internal QA complete: For all selected ideas, thesis, drivers, risks, and valuation fields trace directly to tool outputs and industry/sub-industry benchmarks; momentum and catalyst evidence drawn from weekly returns, factor scores, and recent fundamental updates. Any missing or unavailable data is labeled 'unknown.' JSON fields conform strictly to required schema.

tasks.description: Formatting and delivery. Produce the final output strictly as a JSON array of recommendation objects matching the provided schema, with no additional commentary or formatting. Validate that the JSON parses and adheres to the field requirements.

subtasks.description: Assemble the final JSON array exactly per schema with only the required fields and values; ensure strings are concise, decision-useful, and evidence-backed; place "unknown" where data is missing.
Validate JSON structure (e.g., using a lightweight linter or careful manual review) and deliver the output with no preamble or postscript text.
